description: Use this API to return the number of array entries required to accommodate the data to be returned by the remaining members of the branch flow data family. This API routine was first introduced in release 30.2.0. ierr, brnchs = aflowcount(sid, owner, ties, flag)
python_syntax: ierr, brnchs = aflowcount(sid, owner, ties, flag)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: Defines the bus subsystem to be used. (input; -1 by default).

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: A flag indicating owner usage if ownership is a subsystem selection criterion (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: ties
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem branches to include (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem branches to include. (input; 1 by default).

return_values.name: brnchs
return_values.description: Number of array entries required for the subsystem indicated by sid and owner that meet the editing criteria indicated by ties and flag. (output).

sid.description: to instruct the API to assume a subsystem containing all buses in the working case.
valid bus subsystem identifier. Valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. Subsystem SID must have been previously defined.

ties.description: for each end of interior subsystem branches only.
for the subsystem bus end of tie branches only.
for the non-subsystem bus end of tie branches only.
for each end of tie branches only.
for each end of interior subsystem branches and the subsystem bus end of tie branches.
for each end of interior subsystem branches and tie branches.

error_codes.description: Working case is empty; BRNCHS returned as 0.
Invalid SID value; BRNCHS returned as 0.
Invalid OWNER value; BRNCHS returned as 0.
Invalid TIES value; BRNCHS returned as 0.
Invalid FLAG value; BRNCHS returned as 0.
